result.claim: Albert Einstein won the 1921 Nobel Prize in Physics for his theory of general relativity.
result.executive_summary: Einstein's 1921 Nobel was awarded for the photoelectric effect, not relativity.

sources.title: Albert Einstein – Facts
sources.snippet: The Nobel Prize in Physics 1921 was awarded to Albert Einstein "for his services to Theoretical Physics, and especially for his discovery of the law of the photoelectric effect."

audit.adjudication_summary: All three panelists agree the 1921 Nobel was for the photoelectric effect.

assessments.reasoning: Nobel committee citation is a primary source.

debate_pro.rebuttal: But the citation was for the photoelectric effect.

debate_con.argument: The Nobel citation explicitly names the photoelectric effect.
